problem: The function \[f(x) = \left\{ \begin{aligned} x-2 & \quad \text{ if } x < 4 \\ \sqrt{x} & \quad \text{ if } x \ge 4 \end{aligned} \right.\]has an inverse $f^{-1}.$ Find the value of $f^{-1}(-5) + f^{-1}(-4) + \dots + f^{-1}(4) + f^{-1}(5).$

classified_questions.question: How would you describe the given function $f(x)$ and its components $g(x)$ and $h(x)$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the range of $g(x)$ for $x<4$? How about the range of $h(x)$ for $x\geq4$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the inverses of $g(x)$ and $h(x)$? How did you obtain them?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we compute $f^{-1}(x)$ based on the given function and its inverses?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Given the values $-5,-4,\dots,4,5$, how can we use the inverses to find their corresponding values under $f^{-1}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the expression representing $f^{-1}(-5)+f^{-1}(-4)+\dots+f^{-1}(4)+f^{-1}(5)$? Can you simplify it?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final result of $f^{-1}(-5)+f^{-1}(-4)+\dots+f^{-1}(4)+f^{-1}(5)$?
classified_questions.label: b
